page: Amazon Web Services im4gn.2xlarge is a storage-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an ARM64 architecture with an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The CPU allocation is dedicated, providing 1.0 thread per physical core. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB per core, and a bundled 3750 GB local NVMe SSD. Network performance is supported by a 12 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark results indicate average performance across several core metrics. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests scored 1519.78 and 12131.82 respectively, placing them in the average tier. Memory bandwidth is robust, reaching 472.87 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 121.23 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, single-threaded operations show some limitations, with weak performance in Geekbench single-core at 937.0 and poor performance in bzip3 single-threaded decompression at 7.19 GB/sec. Database operations are stable, with Redis SET operations achieving 9.97 million operations per second and Passmark database operations scoring in the average range.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the im4gn.2xlarge offers a high density of local NVMe SSD storage relative to its 8-vCPU compute footprint, making it a resource-dense option for storage-heavy workloads without the overhead of larger compute instances. Given its high-speed local storage and average database performance, this instance is well-suited for database hosting, caching layers like Redis, static web serving, and applications requiring high-speed sequential read/write access to local disks. It is less suited for compute-intensive tasks, single-threaded compression, or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services im4gn.2xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 32.0 GB of RAM, a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and a bundled 3750 GB local NVMe SSD. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers dedicated CPU allocation with one thread per core. Benchmark data shows average multi-core CPU and database performance, with Redis SET operations reaching 9.97 million operations per second and cached small-block memory reads peaking at 472.87 GB/sec. However, single-threaded performance is weaker, as seen in its Geekbench single-core score of 937.0. Qualitatively, the server provides high storage density relative to its compute resources, making it highly cost-efficient for storage-intensive applications. It is best suited for database hosting, caching layers, static web serving, and workloads requiring high-capacity local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services im4gn.2xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized instance featuring 8 Graviton vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and a 3750 GB local NVMe SSD, designed for database and storage-intensive workloads.

tagline: An ARM64 storage-optimized instance featuring high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage and balanced memory for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 cores at 2.5 GHz
Bundled 3750 GB high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
32.0 GB of system memory with 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average database performance with 9.97 million Redis SET operations per second
Optimized for database hosting, caching layers, and static web serving
